logic_gaps.description: convergence.md has a 3-way within-doc contradiction on the legal-stop gate bundle: (a) §Section-1 blocked_stop event example at lines 105-117 lists 7 gates with Gate suffix (convergenceGate, dimensionCoverageGate, p0ResolutionGate, evidenceDensityGate, hotspotSaturationGate, claimAdjudicationGate, fixCompletenessReplayGate); (b) §6 LEGAL-STOP GATE BUNDLE table at lines 404-410 lists 6 gates WITHOUT Gate suffix (findingStability, dimensionCoverage, p0Resolution, evidenceDensity, hotspotSaturation, fixCompletenessReplay) — note: claimAdjudicationGate is MISSING from this table; (c) §6 buildReviewLegalStop pseudocode at lines 415-444 implements only the 6 gates from the table, also missing claimAdjudication. The event-example is the AUTHORITATIVE shape (reducer reads it verbatim per the §Section-1 prose), so the §6 table + impl pseudocode drift away from the load-bearing event shape. RESOLUTION at synthesis: rewrite §6 table + impl to match the 7-gate event shape with Gate suffix.

loop_protocol.md has a within-doc gate-count drift: §Step-2 Check Convergence (lines 182-186) states the decision tree has '5 gates' but §Step-4a Claim Adjudication (line 351) describes claimAdjudicationGate as a hard STOP gate consulted by the legal-stop decision tree, implying at least 6 gates. Either Step-2 count is stale (should be 6+) or Step-4a's positioning of claimAdjudicationGate as part of the decision tree is wrong.

novel_findings: Iter 9 surfaces 2 within-doc contradictions, both about gate-count drift. LG-0031 P0 is the most consequential gap of phase 5 because the event-shape is load-bearing (reducer reads gate names verbatim), and the §6 table + impl describe a different model than the event example shows.

LG-0031 + LG-0032 + LG-0013 (extended gates not in feature_catalog) + LG-0016 (DRV-018 quality gates count drift) form a cluster: the legal-stop gate model has accumulated drift across SKILL.md, convergence.md (within-doc), loop_protocol.md (within-doc), feature_catalog/, and DRV-018 scenario. A follow-on packet should reconcile all of these against the AUTHORITATIVE event shape (7 gates with Gate suffix).

Iter 9 found ONLY within-doc drift — zero new cross-surface or doc-vs-code drift. Iters 8 + 9 together confirm the SEVERITY MODEL is internally consistent but the GATE MODEL is broadly drifted. This is the strongest convergence signal of phase 5: we've identified the load-bearing root-cause cluster.
notes: Iter 9 RQ: within-doc consistency. Wall-clock 100s. 2 novel gaps, both gate-count drift. LG-0031 P0 confirmed via direct read of convergence.md lines 98-123 + 400-450 — event example uses 7 Gate-suffix names, table+impl use 6 non-Gate names + miss claimAdjudication. Reducer reads event shape verbatim, so §6 table + impl are the drifted surfaces. Connects to LG-0013 + LG-0016 to form a gate-model cluster (4 of 32 gaps about the same root cause).
